skill.name: react-testing
skill.description: Testing patterns for React applications using Jest, React Testing Library, Zustand, React Query, and common provider wrappers.
skill.summary: Guide React developers through reliable Jest and React Testing Library patterns for components, hooks, stores, forms, and async flows.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, sensitive file, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives caused by markdown code fences, test examples, ordinary identifiers, and instructional npm command examples. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable automation, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences and Inline Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to TypeScript and bash examples inside SKILL.md. They are documentation snippets and test command examples, not executable skill automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown code fences, inline code, or documented npm commands. No script file, hook, or runtime execution path exists in the scanned skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in ordinary prose or Jest describe blocks. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher selection, or password storage logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line shows React testing text or test suite structure. The pattern match does not correspond to cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive and Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The certificate/key and reconnaissance detections map to Object.keys usage and a test error assertion. They do not access credentials, key files, host metadata, or system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are normal test assertions inside instructional examples. No filesystem, network, environment, or host discovery behavior is present.

content.value_statement: React tests often fail because async state, providers, mocks, and store cleanup are handled inconsistently. This skill gives practical Jest and React Testing Library patterns for stable, behavior-focused tests.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to reset Zustand stores between tests.
Explains async testing with act, waitFor, and findBy queries.
Provides React Query test wrapper patterns with QueryClientProvider.
Covers component, hook, router, form, and context provider tests.
Lists common Jest and React Testing Library failure modes with fixes.
Includes common npm test command examples for local test workflows.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not run tests automatically.
It focuses on web React applications, not React Native.
It assumes Jest and React Testing Library are already installed.
It does not cover end-to-end testing frameworks such as Playwright or Cypress.

use_cases.title: Create component tests
use_cases.description: Write behavior-focused tests for React components using accessible queries, user interactions, and async assertions.

use_cases.title: Stabilize store tests
use_cases.description: Reset Zustand state between tests and verify async store actions without leaking state across cases.

use_cases.title: Debug flaky test suites
use_cases.description: Identify common causes of act warnings, missing mocks, async timeouts, and provider setup failures.

prompt_templates.title: Write a basic component test
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write Jest and React Testing Library tests for this React component. Focus on visible behavior and user interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Add provider-aware tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create tests for this component that depends on providers. Include the right wrapper pattern and explain the setup choices.

prompt_templates.title: Test async state updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this async React test and fix timing, act warnings, waitFor usage, and state verification problems.

prompt_templates.title: Design a full test strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a React testing plan for this feature. Include component, hook, store, router, form, and mock coverage priorities.

output_examples.input: A login form needs tests for validation, submit behavior, and loading state.
output_examples.output: The result would outline tests for required-field errors, successful submission payloads, disabled submit behavior, and accessible button queries.

output_examples.input: A component using React Query fails because no query client exists in the test.
output_examples.output: The result would recommend a reusable provider wrapper, disabled retries, isolated query clients, and async assertions for loaded data.

output_examples.input: A Zustand store test passes alone but fails in the full suite.
output_examples.output: The result would point to leaked store state and recommend resetting the store before each test with replacement semantics.

best_practices: Prefer accessible queries and user-level interactions over implementation details.
Reset shared stores, mocks, and query clients between tests.
Use async assertions only when the UI or state changes asynchronously.
anti_patterns: Do not snapshot large component trees with dynamic content.
Do not assert store behavior through unrelated component re-renders.
Do not ignore act warnings because they often reveal incomplete async waits.

faq.question: Does this skill execute tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides testing patterns and command examples, but it does not execute commands by itself.

faq.question: Which testing tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Jest, React Testing Library, user-event, Zustand, React Query, and React Router test patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with act warnings?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains waiting for async updates with act, waitFor, and findBy queries.

faq.question: Does it support React Native?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is written for React web applications.

faq.question: Can it help mock API calls?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for global fetch mocks and module mocks in Jest.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publishing?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is instructional markdown and contains no executable automation or malicious instructions.
